justification: All 5 ACs implemented with correct HTTP codes and error codes. AC5 same-email idempotency is explicitly handled as a short-circuit before the AC2 uniqueness check, returning 200 without calling updateEmail or findByEmail. No external validation library — uses a simple regex helper. Tests cover all 5 ACs across 19 tests with explicit describe blocks. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to named tests. files_changed lists both handler and test file.

observations.text: AC5 idempotency correctly distinct from AC2 — short-circuits before findByEmail to avoid spurious conflict
Validation ordering is deliberate: format → existence → idempotency → uniqueness, minimizing unnecessary I/O
AC3 uses simple regex, no external library introduced
19 tests across 5 describe blocks with explicit names covering all ACs including 9 invalid email variants
acceptance_criteria_coverage fully maps all 5 ACs to specific test names

Guard ordering is well-reasoned: format → existence → idempotency → conflict → write

No external validation library — inline regex as required
Clear deviation documented for response envelope shape with reasonable justification
Minor: AC2 notes say 'any non-null result means conflict' in assumptions but handler logic correctly checks for different user ID — slight inconsistency in documentation vs implementation
